citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
A female black and tan, six-year-old Yorkshire Terrier (“Joey Leguire” microchip# 605*259*071) has poor dental health.
Approximately, half the surface of the upper left canine tooth is covered in a thick buildup of grey colored organic material.
Almost the entire surface of the upper left premolars and molars are covered in a thick buildup of grey colored organic
material. Above one premolar the gums appear reddened and swollen. Three quarters of the upper right canine tooth and
upper right incisors are covered in a thick buildup of grey colored organic material and appear blackened with recessed
gums. Almost the entire surface of the upper right premolars and molars are covered in a thick buildup of grey colored
organic material. The condition of the dog's teeth has not been identified by the facility, and no treatment has been
provided.
A female orange sable, nine-year-old Pomeranian (“Grassy Ridge” microchip #s 608*267*007/991001002905801) has
poor dental health.
Approximately one quarter of the surface of the upper left canine tooth is covered in a thick buildup of dark grey colored
organic material. Almost the entire surface of two of the upper left premolars and molars are covered in a thick buildup of
grey colored organic material and appear to have swollen gums. The condition of the dog's teeth has not been identified
by the facility, and no treatment has been provided.
The licensee stated that she checks the dogs’ teeth approximately every six months. The licensee also stated she was
unaware of the dogs’ poor dental health. Poor dental health can cause pain and discomfort and lead to further health
issues. Each dealer must use appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. The
licensee must have this dog evaluated by a veterinarian for an accurate diagnosis and to develop an effective treatment
plan. The licensee must also ensure that each dog receives adequate veterinary care at all times. To be corrected by: The
licensee was verbally told these dogs needed to be evaluated by a veterinarian by 9-June-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n
